page: Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture using the AWS Neoverse-V1 (ARMv8) model running at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and does not include a GPU. Network connectivity is supported by a 22 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark testing, the m7g.12xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory performance. The system achieved a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score, supported by strong memory bandwidth results, including 4652.22 GB/sec for cached read-only small blocks and 252.61 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Multi-core performance is highly competitive, with strong results in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation using Clang. Database operations and Redis SET operations also register strong throughput. Conversely, single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls within the average range, and text generation for small LLM models is relatively weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64-based Graviton architecture offers high resource density and strong multi-core performance per resource unit compared to traditional x86 architectures. The lack of bundled local storage and GPU hardware makes it a cost-effective choice for workloads that rely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound processing. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and strong database, web serving, and multi-threaded execution profiles, the m7g.12xlarge is highly suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation pipelines, and general-purpose containerized applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the ARM64-based AWS Graviton processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores (AWS Neoverse-V1) running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks highlight top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core capabilities, particularly in software compilation, database operations, and Redis throughput, though single-core performance remains average. The m7g.12xlarge is highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, making it well-suited for database hosting, static web serving, and CI/CD compilation pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server with 48 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 192 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server featuring 48 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 48-core ARM64 server delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and strong multi-core performance for balanced enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz.
Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, achieving top-tier memory benchmark scores.
Features a 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Delivers strong performance in database operations, Redis, and software compilation.
Best suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and containerized applications.
